mismatches.got: The formula =IF(A323="E","",MAX(B$7:B377)+1) causes a circular reference because the range B$7:B377 includes B323 itself (row 323 is within rows 7-377). Due to the circular reference, Excel resolves B323 to 0, and the cell number format "000" displays it as 000. Pressing F9 evaluates the formula as a snapshot without circular dependency, so it correctly computes MAX(B$7:B377)+1 = 605+1 = 606.
Blue, Black White, Green, Red Purple, Orange, Brown
mismatches.expected: Blue, Black White, Green , Red Purple, Orange, Brown

mismatches.got: Dim sh1 As Worksheet: Dim lastrow As Long
For Each cell In Range("A1:A" & Cells(Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row)
The macro has three key errors:
1. Single quotes ('A') are used instead of double quotes ("A") for string literals in VBA.
2. Columns(I, 1) is incorrect; Columns only accepts a single argument. It should be Cells(I, 1) to reference a cell.
3. SpecialCells(xlCellTypeBlanks) applied to a single cell in a loop is inefficient and unreliable.
The loop iterates row by row but references columns instead of cells, so no blank rows are found or deleted.

Sub DeleteOldDateColumns()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim col As Long
    Dim currentMonth As Long
    Dim currentYear As Long
    Dim cellDate As Date
    
    Set ws = ActiveSheet
    currentMonth = Month(Date)
    currentYear = Year(Date)
    
    For col = ws.Cells(1, ws.Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column To 1 Step -1
        If IsDate(ws.Cells(1, col).Value) Then
            cellDate = ws.Cells(1, col).Value
            If (Year(cellDate) < currentYear) Or _
               (Year(cellDate) = currentYear And Month(cellDate) < currentMonth) Then
                ws.Columns(col).Delete
            End If
        End If
    Next col
End Sub
Dim codeRow As Long, i As Long
For i = 1 To Rows.Count: If Cells(i, 1).Value = "Code" Then codeRow = i: Exit For: Next i
For i = codeRow - 1 To 1 Step -1: If Application.CountA(Rows(i)) = 0 Then Rows(i).Delete: Next i
